description: Knowledge graph schema for a personal dream journal system with AI-powered extraction and pattern querying.
design_principles: Null is valid everywhere except required fields. Absence of a value is itself information.
is_recurring and is_novel are user-asserted only. The system surfaces frequency data separately and never infers these flags.
co_occurs_with and activates edges are auto-calculated. All other edges are asserted by the user or LLM extraction, with source tracked.
Extraction runs once per entry after the conversation closes, never mid-conversation. User reviews before writing to the graph.

Dream.description: A single dream journal entry. The central hub node — all other nodes connect through it.

date.note: Everything else depends on this. Non-negotiable.

notable_color.note: Free text. Only populate when a specific color is conspicuous. Null is meaningful.

language.note: Primary language of the dream. Null for most entries. Populate when notable.

is_recurring.note: User-asserted only. Never inferred by the system.

recurring_series_name.note: User-defined name for the recurring dream series. e.g. 'My duplex is way bigger and more messed up than I realized'

status.note: For recurring dreams. Dormant = no longer having it. Resolved = consciously processed.

is_novel.note: User-asserted. Marks dreams with a genuinely new creative or architectural quality not seen before.

age_range.note: For pre-journal dreams where an exact date is unavailable. e.g. 'ages 4-8'

title.note: Short evocative title. LLM-generated at extraction time; user-editable before confirming.

Character.description: Any figure appearing in a dream — real people, animals, creatures, or constructed beings. Previously 'Person' — renamed to reflect that dream figures include non-human entities.

character_type.note: real_current = active relationship; real_historical = real person but functionally symbolic; constructed = no real-world referent

symbolic_role.note: Free text. Holds interpretive complexity that categorical fields cannot.

language_spoken.note: Only populate when the language a character speaks is notable.

Symbol.description: A recurring object, image, motif, action, sound, or sensory element that carries symbolic weight.

Setting.description: Where the dream takes place. Promoted to its own node type because location carries as much interpretive weight as any symbol.

real_or_constructed.note: composite = assembled from multiple real sources, e.g. childhood bedroom + grandparents house

Emotion.description: A felt state within the dream. Can be stated by the user or inferred by the LLM. Confidence field prevents inferred emotions from being presented as fact.

confidence.note: stated = user named it; inferred = LLM read it from narrative; ambiguous = unclear

Theme.description: A higher-order interpretive pattern. Can originate from the user or be surfaced by the LLM. Source is always tracked.

LifeContextWindow.description: A persistent waking life context that can move between states of prominence over time. Not tied to a fixed date range — a context can return to foreground after being dormant. Updated occasionally, not per dream entry.

end_date.note: Rarely used. Most contexts don't have a clean end date. Archiving the context is the preferred way to mark it as past.

description.note: Free-text journal entry about this context — what it is, why it matters, how it feels. Written in the user's own voice.

summary.note: Auto-generated 2-3 sentence summary of description. Used in dream analysis prompts instead of the full description to keep context concise. Regenerated when description is edited.

status.note: foreground = active and prominent; background = present but not central; dormant = not currently active; archived = past, closed out.

BodySensation.description: A physical sensation experienced in the dream. Separate from Symbol and Emotion because some dreams are primarily somatic — the sensation IS the content, not a visual symbol.

occurred_during.description: Connects a dream to the waking life context window active at its date.
occurred_during.example_query: Show me all dreams during the job search period.

contains.description: A symbol appears in this dream.
contains.example_query: All dreams where navigation failure was a central symbol.

features.description: A character appears in this dream.
features.example_query: All dreams where mom was active vs. just present.

takes_place_in.description: The dream occurs in this setting.
takes_place_in.example_query: Childhood home across dreams — how distorted each time?

evoked.description: This emotion was present in the dream.
evoked.example_query: All dreams evoking dread — what anchored it each time?

expresses.description: This dream expresses a higher-order theme. Theme provenance (user vs. llm) is tracked on the Theme node via theme.source — not on this edge. An edge-level source property is not used because NetworkX's node_link serialization reserves 'source' for the origin node ID.
expresses.example_query: All regression anxiety dreams, strongest first.

includes_sensation.description: A physical sensation was central to this dream.
includes_sensation.example_query: All dreams where a physical sensation was the primary content.

contributes_to.description: This symbol contributes to or frequently co-occurs with this theme.
contributes_to.example_query: What symbols cluster around legitimacy anxiety?

co_occurs_with.description: These two symbols appear in the same dream. Auto-calculated — any two symbols sharing a Dream node get this edge, frequency increments each time.

frequency.note: Auto-calculated. Increments each time both symbols appear in the same Dream.

co_occurs_with.example_query: Every time navigation failure appears, what else shows up?

associated_with.description: This character's appearances tend to co-occur with this theme.
associated_with.example_query: When mom appears, what themes does she bring?

represents.description: This character symbolically represents this symbol or concept.
represents.example_query: What symbols does mom connect to across dreams?

evokes.description: This setting tends to evoke or co-occur with this theme.
evokes.example_query: Childhood home — what themes does it anchor?

related_to.description: Two themes are related — they reinforce each other, contrast, or one evolves into the other over time.
related_to.example_query: Does navigation failure reinforce legitimacy anxiety, or is one upstream?

activates.description: This life context window tends to activate this theme. Auto-derived by analyzing which themes cluster within a context window.

frequency.note: Auto-calculated from Dream → Theme edges within this context window.

activates.example_query: Which themes spike during major transition periods vs. stable periods?

recurs_as.description: User-asserted link between dreams in a recurring series. Never inferred by the system.

source.note: Always user_asserted. System never creates this edge automatically.

recurs_as.example_query: Show me the full history of the duplex-is-bigger dream series.
